vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry identifies P1 (Yellow Pot), P2/P4 (Black Pot), and P3 (Black Pot with Rabbit). The visual audit confirms the presence of these containers, but the biological contents are in a state of severe decline.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Present (Yellow Pot). Occupant: String of Nickels. Status: Minimal foliage, high senescence.
inventory_reconciliation.P2/P4: Present (Black Pot). Occupant: Mexican Mint/Silver Guest. Status: Significant systemic loss; primary stems appear desiccated.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Present (Black Pot). Occupant: Pothos. Status: Severe chlorosis and necrosis of the primary leaf.
inventory_reconciliation.Systemic_Loss: P3 (Pothos) is experiencing terminal leaf necrosis. P2/P4 shows near-total loss of viable vegetative structure.

plant_audit.P1_String_of_Nickels: Shows minimal change; remains in a state of stasis with very low vitality.
plant_audit.P2_P4_Mexican_Mint_Silver_Guest: The central stem has collapsed. No active growth detected over the 5-day period.
plant_audit.P3_Pothos: The large leaf has transitioned from chlorotic (yellowing) to necrotic (browning/crisping) over the observed sequence.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Soil appears consistently dry across all pots with no evidence of recent irrigation.
biome_observations.incidental_growth: None observed. The substrate appears sterile and depleted.
biome_observations.biome_anomalies: White debris (likely perlite or mineral salt buildup) is visible on the surface of P3. No fungal activity detected.

vision_report.temporal_deltas: From T-5 to Current, the primary Pothos leaf in P3 has undergone rapid senescence, losing its green pigment and structural integrity. The P2/P4 pot has remained largely unchanged, indicating a lack of recovery or active growth.
vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of critical decline. The lack of turgor pressure in the Pothos leaf and the browning of the P2/P4 stems suggest severe water stress or root system failure. The environment is currently non-conducive to plant health.
vision_report.anomalies: The white rabbit anchor in P3 is the only stable element; the biological components are failing to maintain homeostasis.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical audit reveals a failing indoor ecosystem. The Pothos (P3) is undergoing terminal necrosis, likely due to prolonged desiccation. The other specimens (P1, P2, P4) are in a state of arrested development or death. No new growth or recovery is evident across the 5-day observation window.
